title: filter parameter doesn't work with podman image prune
body:
### Issue Description

I found out, podman image prune does not react to filter.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

$podman image inspect 87deb5e9fa81
```
...
          "Labels": {
               "NO": "",
               "io.buildah.version": "1.38.0"
          },
...
```

commands for print images are correct. 

$podman images --filter "label=NO"
```
REPOSITORY            TAG         IMAGE ID      CREATED         SIZE
localhost/runner-ksj  latest      87deb5e9fa81  14 minutes ago  90 MB
```

$podman images --filter "label!=NO"
```
REPOSITORY                         TAG         IMAGE ID      CREATED       SIZE
```

But command for prune doesn't do anything
``` 
podman image prune -f --filter "label=NO"
podman image prune -f --filter "label!=NO"
```

With additional -a parameter to both commands the image is deleted in both commands regardless to filter.


### Describe the results you received

podman image prune ignores the filter

### Describe the results you expected

podman image prune doesn't ignore filter.

I'm looking for solution, some tagged (or labeled) images not to be deleted, but other images yes.

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body: A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Seems this bug has no priority...

Best way to move an issue along is to open a PR to fix it.  Podman has hundreds of issues, and the core team is very busy. Often issues do not rise to the level of importance to get someone to look at them.

I looked a little into this, and it seems docker works the same way. 

podman image prune --filter label=no is looking for images with

`--filter label=no` and `--filter dangling=yes`. I think an image without a name on it is considered not dangling.

$ podman images --filter "dangling=yes" --filter "label=NO"

Should show you no images.

If you execute the following it should work.

```
$ podman image prune -f --filter "label=NO" --filter "dangling=no"
```
Should do what  you want, 

or 
```
$ podman untag IMAGE
$ podman image prune -f --filter "label=NO" 
```
Will work.

@rhatdan Thanks for Help.

Just tested and correct command, what I need seems to be:
```
podman image prune -fa --filter "label!=NO" --filter "dangling=false"
```
This command will delete all unused images, except the ones labelled with NO.
